tagline: Work with GitHub repositories from the command line.

global_flags.name: connection
global_flags.summary: Use a saved GitHub connector credential and repository scope.

commands.notes: The connector exposes issue records as an ETL stream; single issue lookup and gh-style field selection are planned for a later direct-read slice.

commands.risk: Creates a visible issue in the configured repository.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
examples: pm github issue create --title "Ship connector commands" --json

commands.risk: Mutates title, body, labels, assignees, milestone, or state on an existing issue.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

commands.risk: Locks issue conversation for repository users.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

commands.notes: Issue deletion is not exposed as a connector write; destructive or non-REST issue management must be reviewed separately.

commands.summary: Manage development branches for an issue
commands.notes: Depends on local git branch workflow and checkout state.
Requires viewer-centric queries that are not modeled by the repository-scoped stream set yet.
GitHub issue pinning is not modeled in the current REST-backed connector surface.
GitHub issue unpinning is not modeled in the current REST-backed connector surface.
Cross-repository transfer is a high-impact workflow and is not exposed by the repository-scoped connector.
The connector exposes PR records as an ETL stream; single PR lookup and gh-style field selection are planned for a direct-read slice.

commands.risk: Creates a visible pull request and may request reviewers.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

flags.name: issue
flags.summary: Issue number to convert into a pull request.

flags.name: maintainer-can-modify
flags.summary: Allow maintainers to modify the branch.

commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

flags.name: maintainer-can-modify
flags.summary: Allow maintainers to modify the branch.

commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

commands.risk: Reopens a previously closed pull request.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

commands.risk: Comments on a pull request (PRs are issues in GitHub's data model).
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

flags.name: pull-number
flags.summary: Pull request number to comment on.

commands.risk: Merges code into the pull request base branch.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

commands.risk: Adds a visible pull request review.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

commands.notes: Requires check-run/status aggregation not modeled by the current repository streams.
Diff output is a patch/binary-like representation rather than a JSON ETL stream.

commands.summary: Check out a pull request locally
commands.notes: Depends on local git checkout state.

commands.summary: Mark a draft pull request ready
commands.notes: Not modeled by the current REST write actions.

commands.risk: Updates a pull request branch from its base branch.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

commands.notes: Requires viewer-centric and branch-aware filtering not modeled by repository streams yet.

commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

commands.notes: Creates code changes and pull requests; not exposed by the repository connector write surface.
The current connector is scoped to one configured repository.
Repository creation is outside the repository-scoped connector and requires explicit product policy before modeling.
Repository deletion is classified as destructive admin and is not exposed as a connector write.
Destructive or admin repository lifecycle changes are not exposed by this slice.
Admin repository lifecycle changes require explicit policy before modeling.

commands.risk: Creates a fork of the configured repository.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

commands.notes: Depends on local git and filesystem state.
Depends on local git state.
Local gh configuration is outside connector metadata.
Executes the fixed GitHub repository contents read endpoint with file content and raw download URLs redacted.

flags.name: ref
flags.summary: Branch, tag, or commit SHA to read from.

examples: pm github repo read-file --path docs/guide.md --ref main --json

commands.notes: Executes the fixed GitHub repository contents read endpoint for directory listings; file responses are rejected.

flags.name: ref
flags.summary: Branch, tag, or commit SHA to read from.

examples: pm github repo read-dir --path src --ref main --json

commands.notes: Autolink writes are not modeled by the current write set.
Autolink writes are not modeled by the current write set.

commands.risk: Adds a deploy key to the repository.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

commands.risk: Deletes a deploy key from the repository.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

commands.notes: Global license template APIs are not repository-scoped connector streams.
Global gitignore template APIs are not repository-scoped connector streams.
Release records are available as an ETL stream; single-release lookup is planned for direct reads.

commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

flags.name: target-commitish
flags.summary: Commit-ish value for creating the tag.

commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

commands.notes: Depends on local files and binary upload semantics.
Downloads binary assets to the local filesystem.

commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

commands.notes: Depends on local artifact files and signature verification behavior.
Workflow records are available as a stream; single workflow lookup is planned for direct reads.

commands.risk: Triggers a GitHub Actions workflow run.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

flags.name: ref
flags.summary: Git ref to run the workflow on.

commands.notes: Workflow enablement is not modeled by the current write set.
Workflow disablement is not modeled by the current write set.
Workflow run records are available as a stream; log and job expansion are not modeled yet.

commands.risk: Reruns a GitHub Actions workflow run.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

commands.risk: Cancels a GitHub Actions workflow run.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

commands.notes: Downloads artifacts to the local filesystem; artifact metadata is available through the workflow_artifacts stream.
Interactive watch behavior is outside connector command metadata.
Actions cache endpoints are tracked but excluded from the current repository connector surface.
Cache deletion is not exposed by the connector write surface.

commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

commands.notes: Cross-repository copy is outside the configured repository connector scope.
Rulesets are available as a stream; single ruleset detail is planned for direct reads.

commands.summary: Check rules that apply to a branch
commands.notes: Requires a constrained direct-read operation with branch input.

commands.risk: Creates repository rules that can affect contribution workflows.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
commands.notes: Connector-native write action; the current gh ruleset surface documents check, list, and view, but not create.

commands.risk: Updates repository rules that can affect contribution workflows.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
commands.notes: Connector-native write action; the current gh ruleset surface documents check, list, and view, but not update.

commands.risk: Deletes repository rules that can affect contribution workflows.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
commands.notes: Connector-native write action; the current gh ruleset surface documents check, list, and view, but not delete.
Secret metadata endpoints require elevated scopes and are explicitly excluded from the current connector surface.
Secret writes require encryption and strict scope policy; not exposed as connector writes.
Secret deletion is not exposed as a connector write.
Actions variable endpoints require elevated scopes and are excluded from the current connector surface.
Actions variable endpoints require elevated scopes and are excluded from the current connector surface.

commands.summary: Create or update a repository variable
commands.notes: Actions variable writes are not modeled by the current write set.
Actions variable deletion is not modeled by the current write set.
Lists Projects v2 for the configured repository owner using a fixed GraphQL query. Private projects require read:project scope.
Requires fixed GraphQL operations and explicit project policy.
Lists items for a Project v2 node ID using a fixed GraphQL query. Private projects require read:project scope.
Lists repository discussions using a fixed GraphQL query. Private repositories require repo scope; public repositories require public_repo scope.
Views one repository discussion using a fixed GraphQL query. Private repositories require repo scope; public repositories require public_repo scope.
Requires fixed GraphQL mutations and approval policy.

commands.summary: List organizations for the authenticated user
commands.notes: Viewer/org-scoped APIs are outside the configured repository connector.
Search APIs are useful direct-read candidates but not stream-backed in this connector yet.
Search APIs are useful direct-read candidates but not stream-backed in this connector yet.
Search APIs are useful direct-read candidates but not stream-backed in this connector yet.
Search APIs are useful direct-read candidates but need pagination/rate-limit policy.
Search APIs are useful direct-read candidates but not stream-backed in this connector yet.
Gists are account-scoped, not repository-scoped.
Gist writes are outside the repository connector scope.
Codespaces endpoints are tracked but excluded from the current connector surface.
Codespaces creation is outside the connector write surface.
Requires local SSH and interactive terminal behavior.
Polymetrics uses its own credential vault and does not manage gh sessions.
Polymetrics credential inspection is separate from gh session management.
Commands must never print stored secret values.
Local gh configuration is outside connector metadata.
Local gh configuration is outside connector metadata.
Browser-opening behavior is local workflow, not connector API behavior.
gh alias management is local configuration.
Extension installation and execution are local gh workflows.
Completion generation belongs to pm CLI shell integration, not connector metadata.

commands.summary: Make an authenticated GitHub API request
commands.notes: Unrestricted raw API escape hatches are explicitly disallowed; any future raw API must be constrained and separately approved.
Viewer-centric dashboard data is not modeled by the repository connector yet.
Account key APIs are outside repository-scoped connector metadata.
Account key APIs are outside repository-scoped connector metadata.
Attestation verification depends on local artifact files.
Copilot CLI behavior is not a GitHub connector API surface.
GitHub Skills are not modeled by the repository connector surface.
Agent task APIs are not modeled by the repository connector surface.

help_topics.name: authentication
help_topics.summary: Use pm credentials for public, token, or GitHub App repository access. Never print stored tokens.

help_topics.name: execution-model
help_topics.summary: ETL commands map to streams. Reverse ETL commands map to approved write actions and keep plan, preview, approval, execute.

help_topics.name: local-workflows
help_topics.summary: Commands that depend on local git, browser, shell completion, extensions, or gh config are documented but not connector-dispatched.

help_topics.name: known-gaps
help_topics.summary: Projects, Discussions, Search, direct reads, and sensitive/admin surfaces are planned follow-up slices.
